behavior: Forages deliberately on trunks, stumps, and fallen wood; uses its heavy bill to excavate large rectangular holes for carpenter ants and other insects. Also drums on dead wood and gives loud, whinnying calls.
field_marks: Very large, crow-sized woodpecker with a long neck and broad wings
Mostly black body with bold white facial and neck stripes
Long, straight, chisel-like dark bill about head length
Stiff, pointed black tail braced against trunks while climbing
Male has a red cheek/malar stripe; female lacks it
habitat: Mature deciduous, coniferous, or mixed forests with large standing dead trees, stumps, and downed decaying wood; also uses wooded areas retaining these features.
